reasoning: The High Yield OAS index recently spiked to 3.73% in April 2025 due to recession fears (as noted in Q1/Q2 news). However, it has been rapidly falling back toward its strong sub-3.00% baseline (it was 2.64%-2.72% between Nov 2024 and Feb 2025). The current momentum is steeply downward, with the index falling from 3.73 to 3.09 by early June, yielding a linear trend of -0.014 per day. Because the market appears to be correcting strongly back to the 2.70% regime, it is highly likely that in the short term (June 15 and July 8) the spread will continue to tighten and be lower than the ~3.09 value recorded strictly around June 8. Therefore, the probability of an "increase" is sub-50% for near-term dates. As the forecast horizon extends to September and December, generic mean-reversion risks and the potential for new macroeconomic shocks increase. Accordingly, following the required rule that uncertainty must increase over time, the long-term probabilities steadily approach 0.50.
